title: pH-Buffered Temperature-Compensated Equilibrium Controller
description: Maintains a target pH (e.g., 7.0) in a stirred buffer solution across varying temperatures (20–50 °C) using temperature-compensated pH control. Acid and base are dispensed in timed micro-pulses via solenoid valves. A DS18B20 probe provides the temperature correction term. All pH, temperature, and dosing events are logged to SD card. pH fault interlock (< 3 or > 11) cuts valves immediately.
objective: Hold pH within ±0.05 units of setpoint across a 30 °C operating range using automatic temperature compensation and bang-bang dosing control, with fault-safe valve shutdown.
constraints: Arduino Uno, pH-4502C module, DS18B20, two solenoid valves, relay stirrer, SD logger; dilute HCl (0.1 M) and NaOH (0.1 M) only; pH OOR fault < 3 or > 11.
supporting_fact_titles: Chemical Equilibrium and Le Chatelier's Principle
design_element_titles: pH Sensor Module with Automatic Temperature Compensation

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Provides temperature reading for pH electrode compensation and logging.
element_usage_notes.pH Sensor Module with Automatic Temperat: Reads bulk pH every 2 s; corrected for temperature before comparing to setpoint.
element_usage_notes.Solenoid Micro-Dosing Valve: Acid valve pulsed when pH > setpoint+0.05; base valve pulsed when pH < setpoint−0.05.
element_usage_notes.Relay-Switched Magnetic Stirrer: Continuous mixing ensures rapid homogenisation after each dose event.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs t, T, pH, acid_pulse, base_pulse for post-experiment analysis.

element_interactions.to: pH Sensor Module with Automatic Temperature Compensation
element_interactions.description: Temperature reading used to compute corrected pH setpoint

element_interactions.from: pH Sensor Module with Automatic Temperature Compensation
element_interactions.description: pH deviation from compensated setpoint triggers acid or base pulse
Dosing pulses logged with timestamp for mass balance audit
